What I do
\n

Your guide through the AI journey.

\n
\n

My focus is helping companies and their teams adopt AI with confidence, backed by more than a decade managing risk, licensing, and data across ASEAN and APAC.

\n
\n\n
\n
\n
\n
\n Flagship\n
\n

AI Training & Workshops

\n

Practical, role-specific workshops and one-on-one coaching that get your team genuinely productive with AI, built around real workflows, not demos. People leave knowing what to use, when, and how to check it.

\n
\n
\n
\n
\n Flagship\n
\n

AI Advisory & Strategy

\n

Guidance for the whole AI journey: where to start, what to adopt, and how to do it safely. I help you separate real opportunity from hype and build an adoption plan that stands up to risk and governance scrutiny.

\n
\n
\n\n
\n
\n Built on deep domain expertise\n \n
\n
\n
\n
\n

Software Licensing

\n

12+ years of audits with the largest publishers across ASEAN, reaching fair, defensible outcomes.

\n
\n
\n
\n

Vendor & Third-Party Risk

\n

Built Deloitte Malaysia's first TPRM practice. I know which risks genuinely matter, and which are noise.

\n
\n
\n
\n

Business Process Improvement

\n

Redesigning and automating the workflows behind the work, building tools and AI assistants that cut manual effort and free teams for higher-value work.

\n
\n
\n

The same risk-aware, data-driven discipline I bring to every AI engagement.

Insights
\n

Writing on AI, risk & adoption.

\n
\n View all on LinkedIn \u2192\n
\n
\n \n
\n Featured \u00b7 LinkedIn\n
\n
\n
\n
\n
AI adoption \u00b7 LinkedIn article
\n

The most dangerous thing about AI isn't hallucination. It's agreement.

\n

We worry about AI making things up. The subtler risk is an AI that simply agrees with us, quietly reinforcing our assumptions instead of challenging them. Why that matters for how teams adopt AI, and how to guard against it.

\n
Read on LinkedIn
\n
\n \n
\n \n 02\n
How good generative AI is depends on you.
Working with AI \u00b7 LinkedIn
AI output reflects the user, not just the tool. How well you describe context, define outputs, and give feedback determines what you get back.
\n \n \n \n 03\n
AI can do almost everything, except what matters most.
AI & judgment \u00b7 LinkedIn
AI can handle almost any task \u2014 but accountability, moral judgment, and genuine empathy remain irreducibly human. As machines grow more capable, leadership becomes more human, not less.
\n \n \n \n 04\n
Founders: why AI license compliance could cost you.
AI & licensing \u00b7 LinkedIn
Open-source AI models aren\u2019t always free forever. Founders building on models like Llama\u00a03 risk surprise licensing costs at scale \u2014 vague terms give model creators real leverage during your growth.

Track record
\n

A foundation that makes the AI advice grounded.

\n

Over 12 years across ASEAN and APAC, my work has sat at the intersection of data, risk, and commercial outcomes, the same judgment I now bring to AI adoption.

Connor

Bringing AI and data analytics to software licensing and compliance across APAC, building automated, AI-assisted workflows that turn complex publisher data into clear, commercially actionable outcomes.

Deloitte
2013\u20132024

Over a decade across software licensing, vendor & third-party risk, and IT asset management. Pioneered SQL-based data analytics to surface license exposure and quantify risk at scale, and built Malaysia's first third-party risk managed-services practice.
